evals.prompt: Write a description for the Hyde Park Loop. Key points: starts at lower Hyde Park trailhead, climbs paved Hyde Park Road to the ski resort, returns on singletrack through ponderosa forest, 6 miles, 1400ft gain, popular on weekends, great early-season option since it's lower elevation
evals.expected_output: A polished trail run description in the site's voice: conversational, honest about the suffering, practical info woven in naturally, wry humor, direct address to the reader

assertions.description: Uses conversational, first/second-person voice rather than brochure-style prose
Mentions distance (6 miles) and elevation gain (1400ft) naturally in the text
Acknowledges the physical challenge honestly (e.g., quad pain, the climb) rather than just cheerleading
Includes the early-season/low-elevation advantage as useful practical info
Does NOT start with a generic opener like 'Nestled in...' or 'If you're looking for...'

evals.prompt: Write a description for Atalaya Mountain. Key points: classic Santa Fe climb every local has done, trailhead behind St. John's College, steep rocky trail to 9121ft summit, 6 miles out and back, 1900ft gain, views of city and Jemez Mountains, can be brutally hot in summer, crowded on weekends
evals.expected_output: A polished trail run description in the site's voice: leads with the iconic local status, captures the suffering and reward, practical timing advice, no fluff

assertions.description: Uses conversational, direct voice rather than formal/brochure-style prose
Mentions elevation (9121ft), distance (~6 miles), and gain (~1900ft)
Captures the 'every local has done it' iconic status — not just a generic description
Gives practical advice about when to go (avoid summer heat, spring/fall preferred)
Does NOT start with a generic opener like 'Nestled in...' or 'If you're looking for...'

evals.prompt: Write a description for Tesuque Death March. Key points: point-to-point requiring car shuttle, starts at Aspen Basin, climbs to Tesuque Peak via radio towers, 4800ft descent to Tesuque village, 9 miles, upper section exposed and technical, snow until June, lower canyon section through Tesuque Creek is beautiful with wildflowers in July, brutal on quads, name says it all
evals.expected_output: A vivid, narrative description that earns the dramatic name, captures the brutality and beauty arc, uses sensory detail and wry humor

assertions.description: The description earns/justifies the 'Death March' name rather than softening it
Captures the arc: brutal upper section → beautiful lower canyon payoff
Mentions the car shuttle requirement as a practical necessity
Uses sensory or physical detail (quads, creek, wildflowers, snow, etc.) rather than vague adjectives
Does NOT start with a generic opener like 'Nestled in...' or 'If you're looking for...'
